label: Writwater Lake
description: Writwater Lake occupies a subsidence basin formed by the continent-scale catastrophe, its dark, mineral-heavy water lapping at shelves of collapsed basalt and vitrified slag. Stone survey markers and law-stamped boundary steles ring the shore, recording competing charters and interdiction clauses issued by successor polities. Reedy shallows give way to sudden depths where drowned ruinwork lies visible on clear days, and the air carries a persistent taste of ash and brine blown inland from fractured coasts. Periodic liminal anomalies in the region are formalized in posted hazard lines that treat the lake as a regulated exposure zone rather than a common fishery.
